title: quercus rubra var. tinctoria
intro: Quercus rubra var. tinctoria, also known as the Red Oak Dye or Inner Bark of Red Oak, is a variety of the Quercus rubra tree, which is native to eastern North America. The inner bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Quercus rubra var. tinctoria include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. In herbal preparations, the inner bark is often decocted in water to create a tea or infusion, or used in tincture form to extract its medicinal compounds.
